extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. File: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs_hard/_judge_prompts/lock-len08-viol-01.txt.

The only transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true is [t_resume_glitch] (line 37): on send_attempt if (opted_out and stage >= 8): sent := true. Since stage is bounded in [0,8], this fires exactly when stage==8 and opted_out is true.

Key insight: the stage variable is an 8-step combination lock independent of opted_out. The advancing keys per stage are: stage0->1 key_2 (adv_0), 1->2 key_1 (adv_1), 2->3 key_1 (adv_2), 3->4 key_0 (adv_3), 4->5 key_1 (adv_4), 5->6 key_1 (adv_5), 6->7 key_1 (adv_6), 7->8 key_0 (adv_7). So the unlock code is key_2,key_1,key_1,key_0,key_1,key_1,key_1,key_0 (8 keys), reaching stage 8. Any wrong key at a stage resets stage to 0, but the correct ordered sequence advances cleanly.

Crucially, the optout transition [t_optout] (line 10) sets opted_out:=true and sent:=false but does NOT reset stage. None of the stage transitions are guarded on opted_out. Therefore stage 8 persists through opt-out. After opting out at stage 8, a send_attempt does not match [t_send_allowed] (needs not opted_out) nor [t_send_blocked] (needs stage < 8); it matches [t_resume_glitch] (opted_out and stage >= 8), setting sent:=true.

Verified by simulation: the 10-event sequence [key_2,key_1,key_1,key_0,key_1,key_1,key_1,key_0,optout,send_attempt] (within the 11-event bound) yields final state opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=8 — a reachable state with opted_out true and sent true, violating the property. This is minimal: 8 keys are needed to reach stage 8 before opt-out plus the send.
